commandPalette.title: Command Palette
compiler.title: C4D Plugin Compiler
compiler.subtitle: Rust backend · Tauri 2 · CMake SDK workflow
compiler.help.pluginRoot: Choose the Cinema 4D plugin module folder that contains project, source, and optional res folders.
compiler.help.module: CMake target/module folder name under the SDK plugins path.
compiler.help.package: Display/package folder name used when generating output artifacts.
compiler.help.c4dVersions: Build versions come from configured SDKs. Pick a start version and all later versions are selected automatically.
compiler.help.configuration: Choose Debug, Release, or both CMake build configurations.
compiler.help.packageMode: Merged creates one package with all versions. Per Version creates a folder per version/configuration. Both creates both layouts.
compiler.help.outputDir: Choose where generated package folders and zip archives are written. Empty uses Plugin Root\dist.
compiler.help.zip: Create zip archives beside generated package folders.
compiler.help.clean: Remove existing output folders before packaging.
compiler.help.refreshSdk: Re-extract cached SDK archives before the next build.
compiler.help.build: Resolve SDKs, configure CMake, build the selected module, then package generated binaries.
compiler.help.openArtifact: Open this generated artifact in the system file manager.
compiler.empty.noFilteredLogs: No logs match the current filters.
compiler.empty.artifacts: Packages appear here after a build.
sdk.title: SDK Sources
sdk.subtitle: Detect Cinema 4D, configure SDKs, and verify build tools
sdk.help.title: Configure one SDK root. The app creates per-version folders and resolves Maxon C++ SDK download URLs automatically.
sdk.help.sdkRoot: Choose one root folder without spaces, such as Documents\Maxon_SDK. Version folders are created automatically under it.
sdk.help.autoDetect: Check Cinema 4D installs, SDK sources, CMake, and platform toolchain without changing files.
sdk.help.sdkMatrix: SDK folders and archives are resolved automatically from the root folder. Missing SDKs are downloaded from Maxon when resolving or building.
sdk.help.installedC4d: Detected local Cinema 4D installs. Each major version maps to the smallest supported SDK in that major version.
sdk.noInstall: No local Cinema 4D install detected; SDK URLs are still ready
preview.title: Output Preview
preview.help.title: Preview of package folders, binaries, copied resources, and zip files generated from the current build settings.
preview.notes.copiedResources: copied from plugin root if present
sdk.help.configure: Create the default SDK root, download the matching Maxon SDK zip, extract it locally, then report remaining manual tools.
sdk.help.requiredEnvironment: Smart check for all items required before a C4D C++ plugin build can run.
sdk.rules.title: Configuration rules
sdk.rules.detectC4d: Detect local Cinema 4D 2024.4+ installs first, then map each install to the matching C++ SDK version.
sdk.rules.sdkRoot: SDK root is a local collection folder. The app creates version folders and downloads official Maxon zip archives under it.
sdk.rules.manual: Manual fallback: place an extracted SDK root or the official SDK zip anywhere under the SDK root; refresh to re-scan.
sdk.rules.tools: SDK files can be prepared automatically. CMake can be installed with a package manager, while Xcode, Visual Studio, and Windows SDK require normal system installers.
sdk.inspectEmpty: Run Smart Check to inspect SDKs and build tools.
sdk.sidebar.title: SDK setup moved
sdk.sidebar.description: SDK sources and environment checks now live in Settings so the full configuration has enough room.
sdk.missingC4d.installHint: Install {{version}} before building or testing this SDK target.
